_docs.purpose: Single source of truth for all advertising on Rochdale Daily. Edit this file through the GitHub web interface; the site updates on the next deploy. Expired ads drop out automatically; empty slots fall back to the built-in 'Advertise here' placeholders.

slots.home-leaderboard: Homepage top banner. HORIZONTAL: 728x90 or 970x90 (mobile 320x50 via image_mobile). Renders up to 120px tall.
slots.home-billboard: Homepage lower banner. HORIZONTAL: 970x250 (mobile 300x250). Renders up to 280px tall.
slots.article-leaderboard: Top of every article. HORIZONTAL: 728x90 (mobile 320x50). Renders up to 120px tall.
slots.article-mrec: Article sidebar. RECTANGLE or VERTICAL: 300x250 square-ish, or 300x600 skyscraper. Renders up to 620px tall.
slots.article-incontent: Inside article body. RECTANGLE: 300x250 or 580x250. Renders up to 280px tall.

placement_fields.id: Unique, permanent, lowercase-with-hyphens. Used in tracking URLs and reports — never reuse an id.
placement_fields.slot: One of the slot names above.
placement_fields.url: The advertiser's landing page. Clicks are tracked then redirected here.
placement_fields.alt: Accessible description, e.g. 'Acme Plumbing — emergency callouts across Rochdale'.
placement_fields.start: First day the ad shows, YYYY-MM-DD inclusive.
placement_fields.end: Last day the ad shows, YYYY-MM-DD inclusive.
placement_fields.weight: Rotation share within the slot. Two ads weighted 2 and 1 split views roughly 2:1.

directory_fields.category: Must exactly match a category heading on the homepage, e.g. 'Plumbers & heating'.
directory_fields.name: Business name shown on the card.
directory_fields.phone: Optional. Shown as a tap-to-call link.

config_fields.tracker_base: Your ads Worker URL, e.g. https://rd-ads.YOURNAME.workers.dev — leave empty until the Worker is deployed; ads then link directly with no tracking.
config_fields.impression_sample: 1 = record every impression; 5 = record 1 in 5 (reports multiply back up). Use 5 on the free Workers plan to stay inside KV write limits.

_docs.orientation_rule: Match the creative to the slot: horizontal banners go in leaderboard/billboard slots; square, portrait or tall creatives go in article-mrec or article-incontent. Any image is letterboxed within the slot's height limit - never cropped or stretched - so a wrongly shaped creative looks small, not broken.

directory.name: EXAMPLE — delete me
directory.blurb: Example listing. Expired dates keep it hidden.
